techniques.evidence_quote: they were able to enter by using the credentials of the Amazon Web Services accounts belonging to Uber and Lynda.com employees
Private GitHub Repo with AWS credentials
gain access to the Amazon servers that stored data for those companies
they then downloaded private customer information
Mr. Glover and Mr. Mereacre, using pseudonyms and untraceable accounts, contacted security officials at Lynda.com and asked for payment in exchange for the data

attack_chain.description: Attackers discovered AWS credentials stored in a private GitHub repository belonging to Lynda.com/LinkedIn employees.
attack_chain.evidence_quote: Private GitHub Repo with AWS credentials

attack_chain.description: Attackers used the harvested AWS credentials to authenticate to Lynda.com's Amazon Web Services accounts, gaining access to the cloud environment.
attack_chain.evidence_quote: they were able to enter by using the credentials of the Amazon Web Services accounts belonging to Uber and Lynda.com employees

attack_chain.description: Attackers accessed and enumerated Amazon servers/storage holding Lynda.com's customer data to locate target datasets.
attack_chain.evidence_quote: gain access to the Amazon servers that stored data for those companies

attack_chain.description: Attackers downloaded private customer information for approximately 9.5 million Lynda.com users from AWS-hosted storage.
attack_chain.evidence_quote: they then downloaded private customer information

attack_chain.description: Attackers anonymously contacted Lynda.com security officials using pseudonyms and untraceable accounts, demanding payment in bitcoin in exchange for the stolen data (extortion).
attack_chain.evidence_quote: Mr. Glover and Mr. Mereacre, using pseudonyms and untraceable accounts, contacted security officials at Lynda.com and asked for payment in exchange for the data
